definitions.text: Darkness where light, particularly sunlight, is blocked.
examples: The old oak tree gave shade in the heat of the day.
Temps rose to a sweltering 40 degrees Celsius in the shade.

definitions.text: Something that blocks light, particularly in a window.
examples: Close the shade, please: it's too bright in here.

definitions.text: A variety of a color, in particular one obtained by adding black (compare tint).
examples: I've painted my room in five lovely shades of pink and chartreuse.

definitions.text: To shield (someone or something) from light.
examples: The old oak tree shaded the lawn in the heat of the day.
We shaded under a huge oak tree.
You'll need to shade your shot slightly to the left.
Most politicians will shade the truth if it helps them.
